should_trigger.prompt: Create a design system for a new B2B SaaS app with semantic tokens, Storybook documentation, and component governance.
should_trigger.reason: The request is about the system process, tokens, Storybook, and governance.

should_trigger.prompt: Migrate our existing React app from hardcoded styles to a token-based design system with component lifecycle rules.
should_trigger.reason: Migration to tokens and governance belongs to design system implementation.

should_trigger.prompt: Define how new shared components should be proposed, reviewed, documented, and published across our SaaS frontend.
should_trigger.reason: This is a governance workflow for the system, not a concrete UI spec task.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Choose the exact typography scale, spacing rules, and color palette for a new analytics dashboard.
should_not_trigger.reason: Concrete UI values belong to specifications.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Reorganize our Next.js folders into feature modules with shared components and barrel exports.
should_not_trigger.reason: Folder and module structure should route to architecture.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Port our Button and Modal components from one project to another and fix the imports.
should_not_trigger.reason: Cross-project component transfer is portability work.

conflicts.prompt: Set up a new design system and also define the initial color, spacing, and typography tokens for the product.
conflicts.resolution: Start with system architecture and governance, then pull UI specs only for the concrete token values.

minimum_output: Token taxonomy and naming conventions
Storybook or documentation workflow
Governance rules for adding and changing components

should_trigger.prompt: Scaffold a Next.js SaaS repo with feature-based folders, shared layers, and barrel exports.
should_trigger.reason: This is a folder and component architecture decision.

should_trigger.prompt: Our component tree is messy. Reorganize it using App Router route groups, co-location, and shared utilities.
should_trigger.reason: The request is about structure and organization.

should_trigger.prompt: Choose between feature-based and layer-based organization for a medium-sized React SaaS with reusable shared modules.
should_trigger.reason: Pattern choice for project organization is the core trigger.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Define the typography ramp, color semantics, and spacing grid for our dashboard UI.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is concrete UI specification work.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Build a design system with tokens, Storybook, and component governance policies.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is system implementation, not folder architecture.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Audit a legacy React module, add characterization tests, and refactor it safely.
should_not_trigger.reason: Legacy refactoring should not route to new-project architecture.

conflicts.prompt: Reorganize the repo into shared and feature folders while extracting common components into a reusable package.
conflicts.resolution: Choose the target architecture first, then move and adapt reusable components into the structure.

minimum_output: Recommended folder pattern with justification
Clear rules for co-location and exports
Boundaries between feature code and shared code

should_trigger.prompt: Define the typography scale, color roles, and spacing system for a B2B SaaS dashboard.
should_trigger.reason: This asks for concrete UI values and rules.

should_trigger.prompt: Specify our responsive grid, dark mode behavior, and WCAG color contrast targets.
should_trigger.reason: Grid, dark mode, and accessibility specs belong here.

should_trigger.prompt: Create a compact and comfortable density spec for tables, forms, and navigation in our SaaS product.
should_trigger.reason: Density and concrete layout values are UI specifications.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Set up Storybook, token governance, and component review policies for the whole design system.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is governance and system setup, not concrete values.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Choose the best folder structure for React features and shared modules.
should_not_trigger.reason: Folder organization belongs to architecture.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Copy a Card component from another app and adapt its imports and design tokens.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is cross-project portability work.

conflicts.prompt: Our design system exists already, but we need exact color, typography, and spacing values for a new admin panel.
conflicts.resolution: Stay in specs mode unless governance or token system changes are also required.

minimum_output: Typography, color, and spacing decisions
Responsive and dark mode rules
Accessibility acceptance criteria

should_trigger.prompt: Port our Button, Input, and DataTable components from one SaaS project to another and fix missing dependencies.
should_trigger.reason: This is direct cross-project component transfer.

should_trigger.prompt: Extract a shared chart card from our dashboard app and adapt its design tokens and aliases in a new codebase.
should_trigger.reason: The task is extraction, adaptation, and reuse.

should_trigger.prompt: Maintain a registry of reusable React components and document dependencies whenever we move them between repos.
should_trigger.reason: Registry and transfer workflow are portability concerns.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Create a brand-new design system with tokens, Storybook, and governance.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is a system build, not cross-project reuse.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Pick the best feature-based folder structure for our Next.js SaaS app.
should_not_trigger.reason: Folder decisions belong to architecture.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Define exact text sizes, spacing tokens, and color semantics for our app shell.
should_not_trigger.reason: Concrete UI values belong to specifications.

conflicts.prompt: Extract common components into a shared library while also reorganizing the repo into feature and shared layers.
conflicts.resolution: If component extraction and adaptation are the operational bottleneck, handle portability first and then align the folder structure.

minimum_output: Dependency resolution plan
Import and token adaptation steps
Registry or README update instructions

should_trigger.prompt: Define bounded contexts, use cases, entities, and repository interfaces for a new billing domain.
should_trigger.reason: This is a classic DDD and layered architecture request.

should_trigger.prompt: Refactor our service layer into Clean Architecture with domain rules separated from Prisma and Next.js handlers.
should_trigger.reason: The user is asking for layer boundaries and domain isolation.

should_trigger.prompt: Write an ADR explaining how our SaaS should separate application services, domain models, and infrastructure adapters.
should_trigger.reason: ADR plus layer separation should route here.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Design the REST endpoints, auth middleware, and webhook validation for our billing API.
should_not_trigger.reason: HTTP-layer concerns belong to API design.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Model the Prisma schema, indexes, and migrations for subscriptions and invoices.
should_not_trigger.reason: Persistence design belongs to Prisma database design.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Gather requirements, define the MVP, and validate business rules with stakeholders before coding.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is pre-code systems analysis.

conflicts.prompt: Design a multi-tenant billing module including bounded contexts, use cases, and its public API surface.
conflicts.resolution: Define domain and layer boundaries first, then expose them through the HTTP contract.

minimum_output: Layer boundaries and responsibilities
Core domain model or bounded context decisions
Rules for separating framework code from business logic

should_trigger.prompt: Design Next.js route handlers for invoices with auth, Zod validation, pagination, and standard errors.
should_trigger.reason: This is explicitly about HTTP contracts and route handlers.

should_trigger.prompt: Standardize our REST API for public webhooks, rate limiting, and multi-tenant authorization checks.
should_trigger.reason: Webhooks, rate limiting, and auth are API design tasks.

should_trigger.prompt: Review our SaaS API and define consistent status codes, error envelopes, and auth middleware patterns.
should_trigger.reason: The scope is API contract consistency.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Create the Prisma schema, indexes, and multi-tenant data model behind the API.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is persistence modeling, not HTTP design.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Decompose the product domain into aggregates, use cases, and infrastructure ports.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is a domain architecture request.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Design an AI chat interface with streaming, memory, and tool transparency.
should_not_trigger.reason: This has nothing to do with REST API patterns.

conflicts.prompt: Build a billing backend with paginated invoice endpoints and a tenant-aware persistence model.
conflicts.resolution: Prioritize the HTTP contract when the user asks for endpoints first, but delegate schema details to Prisma design.

minimum_output: Resource model and route contract
Auth, validation, and error handling rules
Guidance for pagination, rate limiting, or webhooks when relevant

should_trigger.prompt: Model a Prisma schema for subscriptions, invoices, and organizations with indexes and safe migrations.
should_trigger.reason: Schema and migration design are the direct trigger.

should_trigger.prompt: Optimize our PostgreSQL access patterns in Prisma, including select/include strategy and EXPLAIN ANALYZE review.
should_trigger.reason: Query and index optimization belongs here.

should_trigger.prompt: Choose a multi-tenant Prisma strategy and document how IDs, indexes, and seeds should work.
should_trigger.reason: This is DB-level multi-tenancy and persistence strategy.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Design the public REST routes and error envelopes for subscriptions and invoices.
should_not_trigger.reason: That request is HTTP-facing.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Define bounded contexts and domain services for subscription billing.
should_not_trigger.reason: That belongs to domain architecture.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Gather requirements, roadmap, and MVP scope for a new SaaS before choosing the data model.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is systems analysis before persistence implementation.

conflicts.prompt: Design the invoice schema, repository queries, and the API contract for fetching tenant data.
conflicts.resolution: When schema, indexes, and query behavior are central, lead with database design and then shape the API around it.

minimum_output: Schema or relation design guidance
Migration and indexing strategy
Query and multi-tenancy rules

should_trigger.prompt: Write a production prompt for extracting invoice data with a strict JSON schema, fallback clause, and few-shot examples.
should_trigger.reason: This is directly about prompt construction and output format.

should_trigger.prompt: Choose whether this LLM workflow should output JSON, markdown, or XML and document the trade-offs.
should_trigger.reason: Format selection is a core trigger.

should_trigger.prompt: Redesign our prompt so it stops hallucinating optional fields and returns a stable machine-readable structure.
should_trigger.reason: Hallucination mitigation plus structure definition belongs here.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Reduce token usage, choose between RAG and compression, and solve lost-in-the-middle issues.
should_not_trigger.reason: This is context management, not prompt structure.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Design the UI for an AI chat product with streaming, transparency, and persistent memory.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is AI UX and product interface work.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Create Mermaid and C4 diagrams so an agent can understand our architecture faster.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is a diagramming task.

conflicts.prompt: Design a support copilot prompt and also decide how much retrieval context to send on each request.
conflicts.resolution: Start with prompt contract and output structure, then tune context volume and retrieval strategy.

minimum_output: Prompt structure with required components
Explicit output contract or schema
Fallback or uncertainty handling instructions

should_trigger.prompt: Design a customer support AI chat with streaming responses, visible sources, and clear trust signals for enterprise users.
should_trigger.reason: The request is about AI chat UX, streaming, and user trust.

should_trigger.prompt: Implement persistent conversation memory, stop/regenerate controls, and error recovery flows in our SaaS copilot UI.
should_trigger.reason: Memory, control, and recovery are core interface design concerns.

should_trigger.prompt: Plan an AI assistant interface that integrates MCP tools, exposes context in scope, and remains WCAG compliant.
should_trigger.reason: Tool integration, visible context, and accessibility belong here.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Write the system prompt and JSON schema for an invoice extraction agent with hallucination safeguards.
should_not_trigger.reason: This is prompt contract design, not interface design.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Reduce token cost by compressing documents, reordering context, and choosing between RAG and in-context retrieval.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is context management and token budgeting.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Create Mermaid diagrams that explain which services, actors, and data stores our AI system uses.
should_not_trigger.reason: That deliverable is a diagram set, not interface UX.

conflicts.prompt: Design an AI assistant experience for account managers and also define the prompt contract used behind the UI.
conflicts.resolution: When the product surface and user trust are central, lead with interface behavior and integrate prompt design as a supporting layer.

minimum_output: Coverage of the main trust and control pillars
Streaming, memory, and tool-integration architecture choices
Accessibility, transparency, and error-recovery rules

should_trigger.prompt: Create a token budget for our coding agent and decide what belongs in the prompt, memory, and retrieval layers.
should_trigger.reason: This is direct context budgeting and layering.

should_trigger.prompt: Our long prompts keep forgetting constraints midway through. Fix lost-in-the-middle and context drift issues.
should_trigger.reason: Positional bias and drift are core triggers.

should_trigger.prompt: Choose between RAG, in-context compression, and periodic reminder injection for a large documentation corpus.
should_trigger.reason: The choice between retrieval and compression belongs here.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Write a strict XML prompt with required sections and a fallback clause for uncertain answers.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is prompt structure, not context optimization.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Design the chat UI states, sources panel, and stop button behavior for our AI assistant.
should_not_trigger.reason: This is interface behavior.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Create AGENTS.md and reorganize our documentation hierarchy for humans and AI agents.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is documentation architecture, not token optimization.

conflicts.prompt: Rework our docs so agents get the right context with fewer tokens, including better document structure and selective retrieval.
conflicts.resolution: If token limits, retrieval policy, and ordering are the bottleneck, optimize context strategy first and then reshape the docs to support it.

minimum_output: Explicit token budget or context allocation model
Ordering, compression, or reminder strategy to reduce drift
Decision criteria for retrieval, memory, and caching

should_trigger.prompt: Install this skills library globally for Codex, Claude, and Cursor, then verify every runtime without touching the app code.
should_trigger.reason: This is the exact multi-runtime install workflow.

should_trigger.prompt: Set up project-local Claude and Cursor runtimes plus the global Codex runtime for this repo, starting with a dry-run sandbox.
should_trigger.reason: The request is about choosing correct targets and validating install safely.

should_trigger.prompt: Create a copyable AGENTS.md workflow so any agent can install and smoke-test these skills for Codex, Claude, and Cursor.
should_trigger.reason: The deliverable is an operator workflow for multi-IA installation.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Reorganize our README, AGENTS.md, and ADR structure so humans and agents can find the right docs faster.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is documentation architecture without runtime installation.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Reduce token usage by deciding what belongs in retrieval, memory, and prompt layers for our coding agent.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is context strategy, not installer orchestration.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Write a strict prompt template that any installer agent should follow, with JSON output and fallback clauses.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is prompt contract design, not runtime installation.

conflicts.prompt: Install the skills globally for Codex, Claude, and Cursor, and also create the AGENTS.md entry point that explains the workflow.
conflicts.resolution: Execute and verify the runtime installation first; once the install contract is correct, document it for operators and agents.

minimum_output: Correct runtime targets for Codex, Claude, and Cursor
Dry-run or sandbox validation flow before real global install
Explicit verification and smoke-test steps without editing app code

should_trigger.prompt: Create C4 and Mermaid diagrams so our agents can understand the billing architecture and service boundaries.
should_trigger.reason: This is a direct diagramming request for AI comprehension.

should_trigger.prompt: Document which containers, components, and external systems interact in our SaaS using Mermaid syntax optimized for LLMs.
should_trigger.reason: Mermaid and C4 structure are the core deliverables.

should_trigger.prompt: Replace our ASCII architecture notes with diagrams that show actors, services, and data flow clearly for AI agents.
should_trigger.reason: This asks for machine-parseable diagrams.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Define the full documentation hierarchy, AGENTS.md contents, and ADR governance for the project.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is documentation system design.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Gather requirements, business rules, and MVP scope before we model the solution.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is product and requirements analysis.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Design the UX for a chat assistant with visible context, citations, and streaming controls.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is interface design, not diagramming.

conflicts.prompt: Create an AGENTS.md section with architecture visuals and choose the best C4/Mermaid diagrams for agent onboarding.
conflicts.resolution: If the missing artifact is the visual model itself, produce the diagrams first and then place them into the broader docs structure.

minimum_output: Chosen diagram type with scope justification
A Mermaid or C4 representation with clear boundaries and relations
Guidance on where the diagram should live in project documentation

should_trigger.prompt: Set up README, AGENTS.md, ADRs, and module docs so this project keeps usable memory for humans and AI agents.
should_trigger.reason: This is directly about documentation architecture and persistent memory.

should_trigger.prompt: Reorganize our documentation using Diataxis, documentation layers, and docs-as-code governance.
should_trigger.reason: Diataxis and documentation governance are core triggers.

should_trigger.prompt: Create an AGENTS.md entry point and define where architecture, module, and local implementation docs should live.
should_trigger.reason: AGENTS.md and layer placement belong here.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Write a research report with citations comparing vector databases for enterprise search.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is research and report-writing work.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Create C4 diagrams in Mermaid to explain our service dependencies to new agents.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is a diagram production task.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Gather product requirements, BPMN flows, and MVP scope for a new HR SaaS.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is systems analysis, not documentation governance.

conflicts.prompt: Create a full documentation system for the repo and include an architecture diagram strategy for AGENTS.md and module docs.
conflicts.resolution: Lead with documentation hierarchy and governance, then define which diagrams populate each layer.

minimum_output: Recommended documentation hierarchy with ownership
AGENTS.md minimum sections and layer boundaries
Governance rules for updates, ADRs, and single source of truth

should_trigger.prompt: Write a technical report comparing RAG and fine-tuning for enterprise support copilots, with citations and evidence grading.
should_trigger.reason: This is a research report with source evaluation.

should_trigger.prompt: Review our source base with the CRAAP test and produce a structured literature review on observability tooling.
should_trigger.reason: CRAAP and literature review are direct triggers.

should_trigger.prompt: Define the research question, evidence hierarchy, and citation method for a whitepaper about AI transparency.
should_trigger.reason: That request is about research methodology and writing.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Create AGENTS.md, ADRs, and a docs-as-code process for this monorepo.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is project documentation architecture.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Gather requirements, model the MVP, and validate business rules for a new B2B SaaS.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is systems analysis rather than research reporting.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Design a structured XML prompt with explicit uncertainty handling and output schema.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is prompt design, not research writing.

conflicts.prompt: Produce a report that recommends the MVP architecture for a new SaaS, with external evidence and stakeholder-ready justification.
conflicts.resolution: When the main deliverable is an evidence-backed report, start with research framing and pull systems analysis in to structure the recommendation.

minimum_output: Well-scoped research question or hypothesis
Source evaluation and citation methodology
Synthesis with evidence quality or uncertainty clearly stated

should_trigger.prompt: Audit this messy React codebase, find hotspots, and propose a safe refactoring plan before we touch behavior.
should_trigger.reason: Hotspots and safe refactoring are direct triggers.

should_trigger.prompt: Add characterization tests around an untested billing module and then split its god component safely.
should_trigger.reason: Characterization testing plus refactoring of legacy code belongs here.

should_trigger.prompt: Use git history to identify high-churn files and prioritize a refactoring sprint for technical debt reduction.
should_trigger.reason: Git-based hotspot analysis is specific to this skill.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Set up Vitest, Playwright, and a team-wide testing pyramid for a fresh project.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is testing framework strategy, not legacy rehab.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Choose the best Next.js feature folder architecture for a new SaaS app.
should_not_trigger.reason: New-project folder structure belongs to architecture.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Define bounded contexts and use cases for a greenfield billing platform.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is greenfield domain architecture, not legacy refactoring.

conflicts.prompt: Stabilize a legacy checkout module by adding tests and then refactor duplicated logic and prop drilling.
conflicts.resolution: When the system is risky and already in production, lead with characterization-driven refactoring and use testing strategy as the safety mechanism.

minimum_output: Audit of hotspots, smells, or dependency risks
Characterization or safety-test plan before structural change
Incremental refactoring sequence that separates behavior change from cleanup

should_trigger.prompt: Analyze a new B2B SaaS idea, gather requirements, and define the MVP with business rules and priorities.
should_trigger.reason: This is a full systems analysis and MVP definition task.

should_trigger.prompt: Create C4, BPMN, and ER models for our onboarding workflow and validate the architecture with stakeholders.
should_trigger.reason: Requirements modeling and validation belong here.

should_trigger.prompt: Turn stakeholder interviews into functional requirements, non-functional constraints, and a release roadmap.
should_trigger.reason: This is analyst work linking business needs to system scope.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Design the Prisma schema, indexes, and migrations for subscriptions and invoices.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is persistence implementation.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Create Mermaid diagrams optimized for AI comprehension of our existing architecture.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is targeted diagram production.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Write an evidence-backed whitepaper with citations comparing deployment strategies.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is research reporting, not product/system analysis.

conflicts.prompt: Define SaaS requirements and MVP scope for billing, then map the domain into use cases and bounded contexts.
conflicts.resolution: Start with stakeholder needs, scope, and business rules; once the problem is framed, refine the internal domain model with DDD.

minimum_output: Functional and non-functional requirements set
MVP scope or prioritization model
System models or business rules with validation checkpoints

should_trigger.prompt: Set up Vitest, Testing Library, and Playwright for a Next.js SaaS and define the testing pyramid for the team.
should_trigger.reason: This is a direct testing strategy setup request.

should_trigger.prompt: Apply TDD to a new API route and decide which tests should be unit, integration, and E2E.
should_trigger.reason: TDD and test-level selection are core testing concerns.

should_trigger.prompt: Review our coverage gaps and propose test cases for React components, hooks, and route handlers.
should_trigger.reason: Coverage planning and test selection belong here.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Audit a risky legacy module, identify hotspots, and refactor it safely with minimal behavior change.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is legacy stabilization work.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Design the REST contract, auth middleware, and error envelopes for a billing API.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is API design, not testing strategy.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Create the requirements, MVP priorities, and BPMN flows for a new multi-tenant SaaS.
should_not_trigger.reason: That is project analysis before implementation.

conflicts.prompt: Rescue a fragile legacy module by defining the right automated tests and then cleaning up the implementation.
conflicts.resolution: If the immediate blocker is missing automated coverage and test architecture, lead with the testing strategy and then refactor inside that safety net.

minimum_output: Test pyramid or distribution guidance by test type
Concrete tooling and organization recommendations
Representative test cases or CI quality gates
